target.title: feat(cli-output): tool-use injection + rolling-timer indicator for claude-stream-json backends
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** When Claude calls a tool mid-turn on the `claude-cli` backend, the assistant text stream just pauses — often for tens of seconds on `Read` / `Bash` / `Grep` / `WebFetch`. The end user sees a frozen typing indicator and no signal about which tool is running.
- **Why it matters.** Looks like the gateway hung; users abandon turns that are still in flight. Tool calls live in `stream_event` records, never in `assistant_text` deltas, so the existing assistant-stream consumer never surfaces them.
- **What changed.** Two complementary surfaces on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` (gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`):
  1. **In-stream textual marker** via `onAssistantDelta` — `\n\n[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail\n` followed by a rolling `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator that refreshes every 8s, stripped on the next `text_delta` or on turn end.
  2. **Structured `stream: "tool"` agent event** (`{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}`) via a new optional `onToolEvent` callback — consumed by `cli-runner/execute.ts` and forwarded to the agent event bus for downstream tool-tile UIs.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect({backend, providerId})` — no impact on non-Claude CLI backends. Existing `onAssistantDelta` callers are unaffected. `onToolEvent` is opt-in. No tool-output / tool-result event in this PR (the result is reflected in claude's next `text_delta` and the timer-strip handles UX). Tool args sanitized via `sanitizeToolArgs` before either path.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** tool calls during a Claude turn now produce both an in-stream textual marker (with rolling 8s timer indicator) and a structured `stream: "tool"` agent event, when `backend.jsonlDialect === "claude-stream-json"` or `providerId === "claude-cli"`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142, Opus 4.7, live OpenClaw gateway driving the `claude-cli` backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`; live Telegram turn that invokes one or more tools (Read / Bash / Grep / WebFetch); observe the textual marker, the 8s tick refresh, and the marker stripping plus `\n\n` separator when assistant text resumes; verify the `stream: "tool"` agent event in `emitAgentEvent` logs.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Telegram screenshot of a live turn (rolling-timer rendered to the end-user surface): ![telegram rolling-timer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-82285/.proof/telegram-rolling-timer.jpg). Every `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Bash: …` line in the screenshot is one `emitTool` firing in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` against `claude-cli`.
  - Typecheck clean: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`.
  - The markers are an in-flight overlay delivered via `onAssistantDelta`, not persisted to `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl` (which stores claude's structured `result`) or `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` at default verbosity — Telegram is the canonical capture surface.
- **Observed result after fix:** tool calls render as `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail` blocks in the assistant text, with the live timer ticking every 8s. When claude resumes streaming text, the timer is stripped and the text continues from a clean `\n\n` boundary. Agent-event consumers receive a structured `{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}` for each tool call.
- **What was not tested:** end-to-end against a dashboard / external listener for the `stream: "tool"` event surface (event is emitted, visible in `emitAgentEvent` logs, but no downstream UI verified beyond that); Linux/macOS execution (developed on Windows only); bundled MCP tool paths (`server_tool_use` / `mcp_tool_use` content-block types are matched in the tracker but I have not driven a live MCP-tool turn through it); focused unit test for `createClaudeToolUseTracker` is not added yet (see Regression Test Plan).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Adds opt-in `onToolEvent` callback on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` + in-stream textual marker over the existing `onAssistantDelta`.
- Overlap to coordinate: #80046 (CLI tool-event bridge). Maintainers' judgment on whether inline rolling markers ride alongside or after the structured bridge.

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The condition this addresses (no tool-call UX during turns) is a property of the Claude CLI JSONL surface (tool calls live in `stream_event`, never in `assistant_text` deltas), not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` — add a `describe("tool-use tracker", …)` block.
- **Scenario locked in:** simulated `stream_event` sequence — `message_start` → `content_block_start (tool_use, name: "Read")` → 3× `content_block_delta (input_json_delta partial)` → `content_block_stop` — asserts the resulting `ClaudeToolEvent` carries the reassembled `args.file_path`, the right `itemId`, and that the parser's `assistantText` now includes the `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Read: <path>` line.
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode (regression of partial-JSON accumulation, wrong `content_block_stop` dispatch, verbose-gating drift) is fully exercisable by calling the parser's `push()` with a canned JSONL transcript — no live claude needed. Runs in ms.
- **Honest gap:** I have **not** added this test yet. Behaviour is exercised live in my local environment but a frozen-transcript unit test belongs with this PR before flipping out of draft. Flagging as a must-add before un-drafting.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** partial-JSON accumulation in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` could drop or corrupt tool args if `content_block_stop` fires before all `input_json_delta` chunks arrive.
  - **Mitigation:** tracker holds the entry until `content_block_stop`, then parses `entry.input ?? entry.partialJson` via `parseClaudeToolArgs`. The pending unit test (`cli-output.test.ts`) locks in the contract for partial-JSON reassembly.
- **Risk:** rolling-timer `setInterval` leaks if a `content_block_stop` arrives but no subsequent `text_delta` fires (e.g. turn aborts mid-tool).
  - **Mitigation:** `clearToolKeepalive()` is also called on `result`, on `finish()`, and on the next `text_delta`. The parser closure owns the interval handle and explicitly clears it on every terminal path.
- **Risk:** tool args contain sensitive content (file paths, credentials in `WebFetch` URLs, etc.) and surface in both Telegram and agent events.
  - **Mitigation:** `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates and redacts known-sensitive keys before either emission path. Verbose gating (`shouldInjectToolInlineMarkers`) lets sessions opt out of inline markers without losing the structured event.
- **Risk:** the inline marker rides on `onAssistantDelta` — any consumer that strict-parses assistant text as Markdown could choke on the `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator.
  - **Mitigation:** marker uses a deliberately conservative `\n\n[…] 🛠️ …\n` shape that's Markdown-safe (Telegram renders it verbatim). Consumers wanting strict text can ignore by subscribing only to the structured `stream: "tool"` event.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — same allowed-tools set; `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates args before emission.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same tools claude already executes; this PR only renders that they're in flight.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — tracker reads `stream_event` records the parser already processes.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`; non-Claude backends unaffected.
- `onToolEvent` is an additive optional callback — existing parser callers don't need to wire it up.
- No new config, no new env vars. Disable by not subscribing to the new event surface.

target.title: Fix Codex runtime hook and session churn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes two Codex-runtime stability problems reported by users:

- Fixes #76552: Codex no longer installs the default `PreToolUse` native hook relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, avoiding one `openclaw hooks relay ... --event pre_tool_use` child process per native Codex tool call.
- Fixes #76179: Codex keeps the app-server dynamic tool schema stable across owner/non-owner sender flips. Owner-only tools remain visible to Codex so the dynamic-tool fingerprint does not churn, while unauthorized execution still fails closed.

## Root Cause

For #76552, current `main` already avoided relay subprocesses for `PostToolUse` and finalization hooks when no local handlers exist, but `pre_tool_use` still returned `true` unconditionally in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`. Default Codex app-server config could therefore register a `PreToolUse` command hook that shells out through the OpenClaw relay for every native tool use, even when there was no OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy to run.

For #76179, the Codex app-server thread fingerprint includes the dynamic tool registration set. Owner-only tools were filtered out for non-owner senders, so an owner/non-owner context change changed the registered tool names. That can make Codex start new native app-server sessions and, worse, any per-catalog session workaround can split one OpenClaw conversation across multiple native Codex histories.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` now reports local `pre_tool_use` relay work only when `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` is true. `permission_request` remains active because Codex approvals still need the app-server bridge.
- `src/agents/tool-policy.ts` can retain owner-only tools for schema stability while wrapping unauthorized owner-only execution with `Tool restricted to owner senders.`
- `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` exposes that retention option to runtime tool construction.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` passes `retainUnauthorizedOwnerOnlyTools: true` when building Codex dynamic tools, keeping the app-server tool fingerprint stable across sender-owner flips.

## Before / After

Before:

- Default Codex sessions could register a `PreToolUse` relay command even with no `before_tool_call` plugin policy, so multiple native tools could fan out into multiple OpenClaw relay CLI child processes.
- Switching between owner and non-owner senders could change dynamic tool registration and force new Codex native sessions.

After:

- No `PreToolUse` relay command is installed unless there is actual before-tool policy work.
- Command approvals still get the in-process `nativeHookRelay` bridge for `pre_tool_use` decisions.
- Owner-only schemas remain registered for Codex owner and non-owner turns, so the same native Codex thread can resume through interleaved sender-owner changes.
- Unauthorized owner-only tool execution still fails closed.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex runtime process/session churn. Default app-server runs should not install a `PreToolUse` relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, and owner-only dynamic tool availability changes should not restart or split Codex native sessions.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout on May 18, 2026. Private home/workspace paths and auth details are redacted from the older live-run transcript below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: On current PR head `22051881df2bf82332a46ffce4709db1af00a795`, ran the focused verification commands listed below under `Current-head verification`. Earlier on this PR branch, ran real `pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 ...` turns through the Codex app-server harness.
- Evidence after fix: copied terminal output is embedded below under `Current-head verification` and `Live Codex app-server smoke`.
- Observed result after fix: the owner/non-owner flip test starts one Codex thread and then resumes that same thread across both non-owner and owner turns; the dynamic tool schema includes both `message` and owner-only `cron`; owner-only execution remains guarded for unauthorized senders; current-head tests and prompt snapshot check pass.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live external chat-channel ingress scenario that naturally flips `senderIsOwner`; that path needs real channel identity setup. The same owner/non-owner availability flip is covered through the production Codex app-server run path and dynamic-tool construction tests.

No product UI screenshot is applicable here because the bugs are runtime process/session churn in the Codex app-server path. Terminal/runtime output is the relevant proof artifact.

### Current-head verification

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
22051881df2bf82332a46ffce4709db1af00a795

$ git diff --stat upstream/main...HEAD
10 files changed, 253 insertions(+), 41 deletions(-)

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
# no output

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  203 passed (203)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  44 passed (44)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  112 passed (112)

$ env OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).
```

The direct hook relay test needs a loopback listener; inside the Codex sandbox it fails with `listen EPERM: operation not permitted 127.0.0.1`, so that file was rerun outside the sandbox. The outside-sandbox run passed.

### Live Codex app-server smoke

Earlier on this PR branch, I ran two real local Codex app-server OpenClaw turns with the same session id. That verified the actual app-server harness/auth path and same-thread resume behavior:

```text
$ codex login status
Logged in using ChatGPT

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK"

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK2" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK2"
```

## Regression Coverage

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: no-handler relay state no longer enables `pre_tool_use`; `before_tool_call` still enables `PreToolUse`; `after_tool_call` does not accidentally enable `PreToolUse`.
- `src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts`: retained unauthorized owner-only schemas remain execution-guarded.
- `src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts`: Codex-style runtime turns can keep owner-only schemas stable for unauthorized senders.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: Codex dynamic tool construction opts into retained owner-only schemas, and owner/non-owner flips resume one native Codex thread instead of splitting history.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`: side-question/permission-request coverage keeps permission relay behavior while confirming default `PreToolUse` stays empty when no before-tool policy exists.

Broad Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because the Blacksmith/Testbox CLI/env/profile were not available in this environment. The focused affected suites above are green locally, and the latest push triggers exact-head GitHub CI.

target.title: fix(codex): expose sandbox shell tools for ssh backends
target.body:
Fixes #80322.

Summary:
- Exposes OpenClaw sandbox-routed shell tools to Codex app-server as `sandbox_exec` and `sandbox_process` only when a non-Docker sandbox backend is active.
- Keeps Codex-native shell/file tools authoritative for normal workspace operations; `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` stay hidden from OpenClaw dynamic tools.
- Preserves existing `codexDynamicToolsExclude` and runtime `toolsAllow` behavior, including `exec`/`process` aliases for the new namespaced sandbox tools.
- Adds regression tests and changelog entry for #80322.

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` -> 172 tests passed.
- `pnpm -s check:changed` through Blacksmith Testbox via Crabbox -> `tbx_01krxs3kt1znkvjat55a4rz2bw`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26041124573, exit 0.
- `pnpm build` through Blacksmith Testbox via Crabbox -> `tbx_01krxsdk63tvrn0n6b4a080etk`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26041431959, exit 0.
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 AUTOREVIEW_OPENCLAW_MAINTAINER_VALIDATION=1 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --reviewer codex --fallback-reviewer none` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Live Codex SSH sandbox E2E on AWS Crabbox -> `cbx_7582df12c8d0` / run `run_0c68f2e8b5c2`, hydrated by Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26042017213, exit 0.

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server sessions using OpenClaw SSH sandbox routing can now reach an OpenClaw-provided shell without shadowing Codex native shell.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox `cbx_7582df12c8d0` with Docker-hosted SSH target, env-backed live `OPENAI_API_KEY`, Codex app-server runtime, model `openai/gpt-5.5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created an isolated OpenClaw config with `agents.defaults.sandbox.backend="ssh"`, `tools.exec.host="sandbox"`, `codexDynamicToolsLoading="direct"`, and an env-backed `openai-codex` auth profile, then ran `node dist/entry.js agent --local --agent main --model openai/gpt-5.5 --message "Use ... sandbox_exec ... cat /home/sandbox/remote-only.txt ..." --thinking off --json`.

Evidence after fix: The model replied `OPENCLAW_SANDBOX_EXEC_SSH_OK_1779118303-25670`; the test found a Codex app-server binding file and 8 session artifact hits containing `sandbox_exec` or the marker.

Observed result after fix: The live Codex agent used the sandbox-routed OpenClaw dynamic tool path to read a marker that existed only inside the SSH target container, and the run exited 0.

What was not tested: Full suite and a separate live Docker-backend negative run; Docker/no-sandbox behavior is covered by focused regression tests.

target.title: IRC: disable block streaming by default
target.body:
## Summary
- disable IRC block streaming unless `channels.irc.blockStreaming=true` is explicitly set
- keep existing per-account opt-in behavior for users who want streamed IRC replies
- add regression coverage for the IRC block-streaming default

## Root cause
IRC inherited the global block-streaming default when `channels.irc.blockStreaming` was unset. With some models, only the last streamed text block was emitted before the full final reply was suppressed by the block-streaming path, which matches #42121.

## Testing
- `pnpm test extensions/irc/src/inbound.policy.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/irc/src/monitor.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/irc/src/send.test.ts`

Closes #42121

target.title: fix(image-generate): allow distinct active image requests
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: `image_generate` treated any active image generation task in the same session as a duplicate, even when the next call had a distinct prompt/filename for a different image.
- Why it matters: multi-image workflows could silently drop the second image request while returning a status for the first active task, causing agents to report false progress.
- What changed: duplicate guarding now matches the active image task by prompt, so same-prompt retries still reuse active status while distinct prompts can create their own background tasks.
- Docs: the media task guardrail docs now describe the prompt-scoped `image_generate` behavior separately from music/video generation.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider behavior, image output format, media saving, completion wake delivery, or video/music generation behavior is changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83610
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: session-backed image generation duplicate guard dropping a distinct second image request.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw source checkout, session-backed Telegram workflow observed on an existing OpenClaw gateway. Sensitive chat/media details omitted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Created one active session-scoped `image_generation` task in the production task ledger using redacted prompt A.
  2. Called the production `image_generate` duplicate guard with prompt A and prompt B.
  3. Verified prompt A returns the existing active task status.
  4. Verified prompt B is not treated as a duplicate, so the normal tool path can create a new background task.
  5. Ran the focused regression tests for the tool and active-task lookup.
- Evidence after fix (copied local output):

```text
$ pnpm exec tsx --input-type=module - <<'E O F'
import { createRunningTaskRun } from "./src/tasks/task-executor.ts";
import { resetTaskRegistryForTests } from "./src/tasks/runtime-internal.ts";
import { createImageGenerateDuplicateGuardResult } from "./src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.actions.ts";
import { findActiveImageGenerationTaskForSession } from "./src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts";

resetTaskRegistryForTests({ persist: false });
const sessionKey = "redacted-session";
createRunningTaskRun({
  runtime: "cli",
  taskKind: "image_generation",
  sourceId: "image_generate:openai",
  requesterSessionKey: sessionKey,
  ownerKey: sessionKey,
  scopeKind: "session",
  childSessionKey: sessionKey,
  runId: "tool:image_generate:redacted-a",
  label: "Image generation",
  task: "redacted prompt A",
  deliveryStatus: "not_applicable",
  notifyPolicy: "silent",
  startedAt: Date.now(),
  lastEventAt: Date.now(),
  progressSummary: "Generating image",
});

const samePrompt = createImageGenerateDuplicateGuardResult(sessionKey, { prompt: "redacted prompt A" });
const distinctPrompt = createImageGenerateDuplicateGuardResult(sessionKey, { prompt: "redacted prompt B" });
const activeA = findActiveImageGenerationTaskForSession(sessionKey, { prompt: "redacted prompt A" });
const activeB = findActiveImageGenerationTaskForSession(sessionKey, { prompt: "redacted prompt B" });

console.log(JSON.stringify({
  samePromptDuplicateGuard: samePrompt?.details?.duplicateGuard === true,
  samePromptExistingTask: samePrompt?.details?.existingTask === true,
  distinctPromptDuplicateGuard: distinctPrompt?.details?.duplicateGuard === true,
  distinctPromptCanStartNewTask: distinctPrompt === undefined,
  activeLookupPromptA: activeA?.runId,
  activeLookupPromptB: activeB?.runId ?? null,
}, null, 2));
E O F

{
  "samePromptDuplicateGuard": true,
  "samePromptExistingTask": true,
  "distinctPromptDuplicateGuard": false,
  "distinctPromptCanStartNewTask": true,
  "activeLookupPromptA": "tool:image_generate:redacted-a",
  "activeLookupPromptB": null
}

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  33 passed (33)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
```

- Observed result after fix: a distinct prompt is allowed to start a second task even while another image task is active in the same session; same-prompt retries are still guarded.
- What was not tested: a live provider call after this patch that actually generates two paid images. The regression target is the task ledger / duplicate guard before provider execution, covered by the production task-ledger smoke and regression tests.
- Before evidence: in a real session-backed chat, an agent requested two distinct diagrams. The second `image_generate` call returned the first active task's status and did not create a new task for the second image. The first image completed and delivered; the second image only started after a later manual follow-up. Private media IDs and prompts are intentionally omitted.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: active image task lookup was scoped to session/task kind/source prefix only, and the duplicate guard ran before representing the new request as a task. It could not distinguish a same-request retry from a valid second image request.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test covered two distinct image prompts in the same session while the first image task was active.
- Contributing context (if known): `count` supports multiple outputs for one prompt, but there was no representation for multiple distinct prompts/filenames in one workflow.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - distinct prompt while another image task is active creates a new task
  - same prompt while an image task is active returns duplicate guard status
  - active image task lookup can be restricted by prompt
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug happens before provider execution, at duplicate guard / task lookup time.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Agents can start a second session-backed image generation task for a distinct prompt while another image generation task is still active. Repeated calls with the same prompt still return the active task status.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[image A active] -> [image_generate image B] -> [status for image A] -> [image B not started]

After:
[image A active] -> [image_generate image B] -> [new task for image B] -> [both complete independently]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw source checkout
- Model/provider: redacted production task-ledger smoke for after-fix guard behavior; real session observation used for before behavior
- Integration/channel (if any): session-backed chat
- Relevant config (redacted): image generation provider configured

### Steps

1. Have an active session-backed image generation task with prompt A.
2. Call `image_generate` in the same session with prompt B.
3. Observe whether a task for prompt B is created.
4. Repeat with prompt A to verify duplicate guard behavior remains intact.

### Expected

- Prompt B creates a new image generation task.
- Prompt A returns active status for the existing task.

### Actual

- Before this PR: prompt B returned active status for prompt A's task and did not start.
- After this PR: prompt B starts a new task; prompt A remains guarded.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - production task-ledger smoke for distinct prompt vs same prompt behavior
  - local tests for distinct prompt vs same prompt behavior
  - issue reproduction path by source inspection and redacted live-session observation
  - docs updated for prompt-scoped image guard behavior
- Edge cases checked:
  - active task lookup can select matching prompt among multiple active tasks
  - same-prompt retry does not create a duplicate task
- What you did **not** verify:
  - live paid image generation after patch

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: prompt-only matching may allow two very similar but not identical prompts to run concurrently.
  - Mitigation: this is the intended behavior for distinct image requests; exact same prompt retries remain guarded.

target.title: Browser: harden noVNC bootstrap headers
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the loopback-only `/sandbox/novnc` bootstrap page relied on `no-store` and `no-referrer`, but it did not send a document CSP, `nosniff`, or frame-deny headers.
- Why it matters: this page carries the short-lived observer redirect flow, so it should be locked down as tightly as possible even though the route is loopback-only and token-gated.
- What changed: generate a per-response script nonce, attach a restrictive CSP, and add `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` plus `X-Frame-Options: DENY`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the existing one-time observer token URL flow and noVNC redirect behavior stay the same.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #42476

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + Vitest
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Browser bridge / sandbox noVNC observer
- Relevant config (redacted): loopback browser bridge with auth token and short-lived noVNC observer token

### Steps

1. Start the browser bridge with `resolveSandboxNoVncToken` enabled.
2. Request `/sandbox/novnc?token=<valid-token>`.
3. Inspect response headers and bootstrap HTML.

### Expected

- The bootstrap page still redirects to the local noVNC target.
- The response includes no-store/no-referrer plus strict document hardening headers.
- The password remains in the URL fragment, not the query string or `Location` header.

### Actual

- The response now includes a nonce-based CSP, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, and `X-Frame-Options: DENY` while preserving the existing redirect flow.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `/sandbox/novnc` still serves bootstrap HTML, still embeds the target noVNC fragment URL, and now emits the expected CSP / frame / nosniff headers.
- Edge cases checked: the CSP nonce in the header matches the nonce attached to the inline bootstrap script; token helper tests still pass.
- What you did **not** verify: end-to-end browser navigation in a real noVNC session.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commit 4cf57794f1b6c1b3aa1bf7fd0d22ea842c45da36.
- Files/config to restore: `src/browser/bridge-server.ts`, `src/browser/bridge-server.auth.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `/sandbox/novnc` returns 200 but the bootstrap script is blocked by CSP, or headers regress to being absent.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: an incorrect CSP could block the bootstrap redirect script.
- Mitigation: the test now asserts that the per-response nonce is present both in the HTML and the header, and the redirect body content is unchanged.

target.title: fix(feishu): improve external key validation to allow legitimate keys containing '..'
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes #42257 - Feishu image analysis not working

## Problem
The previous implementation rejected any Feishu image/file key containing ".." as a potential path traversal attack. However, legitimate Feishu keys may contain ".." as a substring (e.g., `img_v2_test..key`), causing images to be incorrectly filtered out and preventing agents from analyzing them.

## Root Cause
The security check in `normalizeFeishuExternalKey()` used `includes("..")` which was too broad and blocked legitimate keys containing ".." as a substring.

## Solution
- Refined path traversal detection to only block actual traversal patterns (e.g., `../`, `a/../b`, `..\folder`)
- Split by path separators and check each segment individually
- Allow keys where ".." appears without path separators

## Changes
- `extensions/feishu/src/external-keys.ts`: Improved path traversal detection logic
- `extensions/feishu/src/external-keys.test.ts`: Added regression tests

## Testing
- ✅ All 5 tests pass
- ✅ Verified legitimate keys containing ".." are now accepted
- ✅ Verified actual path traversal attacks are still blocked

## Impact
- Fixes image analysis for Feishu channels
- Maintains security against actual path traversal attacks
- No breaking changes

target.title: feat(image-tool): add imageCompressionQuality and imageCompressionMaxSide config
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #41771

The image tool compresses images automatically before sending them to vision models, but compression parameters were not configurable. This caused detail loss for text-dense documents like contracts and business licenses where high quality matters.

- Add `agents.defaults.imageCompressionQuality` (integer, 1–100): minimum JPEG quality floor. The compression optimizer will not drop below this value. Default: unset (existing ladder floor of 40).
- Add `agents.defaults.imageCompressionMaxSide` (integer, positive): maximum pixel side for the resize ladder. Default: unset (existing ceiling of 2048).

### Example config for document recognition

```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    imageCompressionQuality: 95
    imageCompressionMaxSide: 2000
```

## Changes

- `src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts` — add `imageCompressionQuality` and `imageCompressionMaxSide` to `AgentDefaultsConfig`
- `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts` — add Zod validation for both fields
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts` / `schema.help.ts` — add labels and help text
- `src/web/media.ts` — add `minQuality`/`maxSide` to `WebMediaOptions` and `optimizeImageToJpeg`; thread through `optimizeImageWithFallback` and `loadWebMediaInternal`
- `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts` — read config and pass compression options to `loadWebMedia` calls
- Tests added for `pickCompressionOptions` and `optimizeImageToJpeg` constraint handling

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/tools/image-tool src/web/media` — 60 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm build` — clean build, no warnings
- [x] `pnpm check` — lint clean
- [x] Verify `minQuality` floor is respected: output quality ≥ configured floor
- [x] Verify `maxSide` cap is respected: resizeSide ≤ configured maximum
- [x] Verify fallback to smallest result when constraints prevent hitting size cap

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix: recover stuck Control UI chat runs
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Control UI chat can stay stuck in a busy state after a long-running run if the terminal chat event is missed.
- Why it matters: the next user message gets queued in the UI and does not flush automatically, so the session looks stalled until the user pokes it again.
- What changed: added a small client-side watchdog that probes `agent.wait` after chat inactivity and recovers the UI when the gateway already considers the run finished.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not change backend chat protocol, queue semantics, or model rate-limit retry behavior.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42213

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Stale Control UI chat runs can recover automatically instead of blocking the next queued message forever.
- If `agent.wait` still reports the run as active, the UI keeps waiting and does not flush the queue early.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: local macOS dev environment
- Runtime/container: Node.js dev environment
- Model/provider: not required for the regression tests
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI chat
- Relevant config (redacted): not required

### Steps

1. Start a long-running Control UI chat run.
2. Let the UI miss the terminal chat event and keep `chatRunId` active.
3. Queue another user message behind that stale run.

### Expected

- The UI should probe `agent.wait` after inactivity.
- If the run is already finished, the busy state should clear and the queued message should flush.

### Actual

- Before this change, the queued message could remain blocked behind a stale UI run until the user sent another prompt.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `pnpm vitest run ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`, `pnpm exec oxlint ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`, `pnpm build`
- Edge cases checked: stale finished run recovery, still-active run timeout path, watchdog reset before firing
- What you did **not** verify: a live end-to-end reproduction against a real gateway/browser session after this patch

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR or remove the chat watchdog helpers in `ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts`
- Files/config to restore: `ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.ts`, `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: queued messages flushing while a run is still genuinely active

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the UI now probes `agent.wait` after inactivity and could recover too aggressively if the inactivity threshold is wrong
  - Mitigation: the watchdog only recovers when `agent.wait` reports terminal status, and keeps waiting when the run still reports `timeout`

target.title: Speed up install smoke Docker builds
target.body:
## Summary

Speed up the `Install Smoke / install-smoke` workflow by skipping the full control UI build only for the smoke Docker image builds.

## What changed

- add `OPENCLAW_SKIP_UI_BUILD` in `Dockerfile`
- when that flag is set, generate a minimal `dist/control-ui/index.html` placeholder instead of running `pnpm ui:build`
- pass `OPENCLAW_SKIP_UI_BUILD=1` only from `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yml` for the root/ext smoke image builds

## Why

The smoke workflow only validates that the Dockerfile still builds and that the CLI starts. It does not exercise the full control UI bundle, so paying the full `pnpm ui:build` cost there is unnecessary.

Release and normal Docker builds are unchanged because the new path is opt-in and only enabled by the smoke workflow.

## Testing

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check Dockerfile .github/workflows/install-smoke.yml`

target.title: fix(backup): treat non-existent output paths as directories
target.body:
When the user specifies an output directory that doesn't exist yet (without a trailing slash), the backup command was incorrectly treating it as a file path instead of a directory. This caused the archive to be created without the .tar.gz extension.

Fix: Default to treating non-existent paths as directories by checking for archive extensions (.tar.gz, .zip, .tgz). If no extension, treat as directory.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#41830

target.title: Google: show detailed Gemini CLI OAuth extraction failures
target.body:
## Summary

Improve Gemini CLI OAuth credential extraction diagnostics in the active bundled Google plugin.

This PR now targets `extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts` instead of the removed legacy `extensions/google-gemini-cli-auth/oauth.ts` path.

## What changed

- Added extraction diagnostic state alongside the Gemini CLI credential cache.
- Preserved distinct failure details for:
  - Gemini CLI binary missing from `PATH`
  - missing `oauth2.js` or bundled credential source
  - candidate credential files that cannot be parsed for client id/secret
  - unexpected read/extraction exceptions
- Appended those details to the `resolveOAuthClientConfig()` setup error.
- Reset extraction diagnostic state from `clearCredentialsCache()`.
- Added focused regression coverage in `extensions/google/oauth.test.ts` around `resolveOAuthClientConfig()`.

## Why

Current `main` collapses multiple extraction failures into the same generic install-or-set-env-var message. That is misleading when Gemini CLI exists but OpenClaw cannot locate, read, or parse its OAuth client config source.

## Scope

Diagnostics-only. This does not change the automatic Gemini CLI credential extraction behavior.

#54289 may supersede this diagnostics-only fix if maintainers decide to remove or gate automatic Gemini CLI credential extraction.

## Validation

- `pnpm test -- extensions/google/oauth.test.ts`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts extensions/google/oauth.test.ts`

## AI Assistance

- This PR is AI-assisted (OpenClaw + LLM coding assistant).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Gemini CLI OAuth setup failures should no longer collapse every automatic client-config extraction failure into only the generic install-or-set-env-var guidance; the setup error should include the concrete extraction reason.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 source checkout of this PR branch at `C:\Users\bgmbg\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr-41991-proof`, running Node.js v22.22.0 with tsx against the PR implementation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Checked out this PR branch in the Windows source checkout at `C:\Users\bgmbg\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr-41991-proof`.
- Installed dependencies for the source checkout and ran a live Node/tsx harness against `resolveOAuthClientConfig()`.
- Set `PATH` to `C:\definitely-not-a-real-gemini-path` to simulate an OpenClaw setup where the Gemini CLI binary is unavailable, then called the actual OAuth config resolver from `extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts`.
- Command: `node --import tsx real-proof.mjs`

Evidence after fix:
```text
COMMAND: node --import tsx real-proof.mjs
CASE: missing Gemini CLI binary in PATH
OUTPUT:
Gemini CLI not found. Install it first: brew install gemini-cli (or npm install -g @google/gemini-cli), or set GEMINI_CLI_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID. Details: Gemini CLI binary was not found in PATH during OAuth credential extraction.
```

Observed result after fix:
The user-facing setup error now preserves the actionable extraction reason after the generic setup guidance: `Details: Gemini CLI binary was not found in PATH during OAuth credential extraction.`

What was not tested:
Full interactive browser OAuth login/token exchange was not exercised for this proof; this PR is limited to setup-error diagnostics when automatic Gemini CLI client-config discovery fails.

target.title: fix(web-fetch): cap error detail for access-denied responses (401/403/407/451)
target.body:
## Problem

When `web_fetch` hits a soft-block HTTP response (401, 403, 407, 451) and Firecrawl is unavailable, the error message includes up to **4,000 characters** of stripped HTML body text. For common login-wall pages (Zhihu, Weibo, Medium, paywalled news sites, etc.), this dumps pages of useless boilerplate into the LLM context:

```
Web fetch failed (403): <<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="...">
SECURITY NOTICE ...
知乎让每一次点击都充满意义 — 如果问题持续存在请登录试一下 ...
(~4000 chars of login-wall marketing copy)
<<<
```

The agent already knows the URL and status code — that is all the information needed to decide the next action. The 4,000-char body content provides **zero** actionable signal for access-denied responses and wastes ~875 tokens per blocked fetch.

## Fix

Introduce a tighter cap (`SOFT_BLOCK_ERROR_MAX_CHARS = 256`) for access-denied status codes (401, 403, 407, 451). Other error statuses (404, 500, etc.) retain the existing 4,000-char limit where the body may contain useful diagnostics.

**Changes:**
- `src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ts`: Add `SOFT_BLOCK_ERROR_MAX_CHARS` / `SOFT_BLOCK_STATUS_CODES` constants; select error cap based on HTTP status in `runWebFetch`.
- `src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts`: 3 new tests covering 403, 401 (short cap), and 500 (full cap preserved).

## Impact

- **Tokens saved**: ~875 per blocked fetch (3,500 chars × ~0.25 tokens/char)
- **No behavioral change** for Firecrawl users (fallback fires before error path)
- **No behavioral change** for non-soft-block errors (404, 500, etc.)
- All existing tests pass (17/17 fetch + 5/5 SSRF)

## Testing

```
vitest run src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts  → 17/17 ✓ (3 new)
vitest run src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ssrf.test.ts   →  5/5  ✓
```

target.title: feat(control-ui): add cron calendar timeline view
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a 24h timeline strip to the Cron Jobs page in the Control UI, providing an at-a-glance view of today's scheduled tasks.

Closes #40644

## Screenshots

### Dark Theme — Full View
![Full page dark](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dark-full.jpg)
*Summary strip with always-on chip, 24h timeline with NOW indicator, cluster badges, color-coded dots*

### Cluster Hover — Overlapping Jobs
![Cluster hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/cluster-hover.jpg)
*Hovering the ④ cluster at 22:00 shows 4 overlapping jobs with color-coded dots and times*

### Single Dot Hover — Job Info Popup
![Dot hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dot-hover.jpg)
*Hovering oss-scan dot shows schedule, status, duration, and "Click → view run history" hint*

### Light Theme
![Light theme](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/light-theme.jpg)
*Full light theme support via existing CSS variables*

## Features

### Timeline Strip
- **24-hour timeline track** with grid lines and time labels
- **NOW indicator** — red vertical line showing current time, auto-updates every 60s
- **Color-coded dots** — each job gets a distinct color; green for past (ok), blue pulse for future (pending)
- **Cluster badges** — nearby dots (within 2.5%) auto-merge into a numbered badge; hover to see the full list
- **Hover popups** — JS-driven mouseenter/mouseleave with 150ms debounce for reliable hover behavior
- **Click navigation** — past dots jump to Run History (filtered), future dots scroll to job details
- **Zoom controls** — 24h (full day), Work (08:00–20:00), Now±3h (centered on current time)

### Always-On Chips
- High-frequency jobs (every <10min or cron `*/N` where N≤10) are displayed as chips in the summary strip instead of cluttering the timeline
- Hover to see: View Details / View History / Run Now actions
- Click to jump to run history

### Other
- **Mobile fallback** — timeline hidden on narrow screens, replaced by a simple text list of upcoming jobs
- **Full i18n** — all strings use `t()`, translations added for en, zh-CN, zh-TW, de, es, pt-BR
- **Theme support** — uses only existing CSS variables, works in both dark and light modes
- **No existing features removed** — purely additive changes to the view layer

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ui/src/ui/helpers/cron-timeline.ts` | **New** — Timeline computation (zoom, clustering, high-freq detection) |
| `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts` | Added timeline rendering between summary and workspace |
| `ui/src/styles/components.css` | Added timeline CSS (523 lines) |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/*.ts` | Added `cron.timeline.*` keys to all 6 locales |

## Testing

- Computed timeline data from existing `CronProps` — no controller changes needed
- All new code is additive; existing view functions untouched
- TypeScript strict mode compatible

---

*AI Disclosure: This PR was authored with AI assistance (Claude). All code was reviewed and tested by a human.*

target.title: fix: preserve code block indentation in duplicate block collapsing
target.body:
Closes #41699

## Problem

`collapseConsecutiveDuplicateBlocks()` in `src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.ts` uses `block.trim()` when rebuilding text after removing consecutive duplicate blocks. This strips leading whitespace (indentation) from code blocks, making JSON/Python/YAML output harder to read.

## Fix

Replace `result.push(block.trim())` with `result.push(block)`.

The comparison logic already uses a separate `normalizeBlock()` that trims and collapses whitespace for dedup purposes — the output block itself does not need to be trimmed.

## Impact

Only affects messages where consecutive duplicate text blocks exist AND the text contains indented code blocks. The early-return path (`result.length === blocks.length → return text`) is unchanged, so messages without duplicates are not affected at all.

target.title: fix(gateway): rewrite favicon hrefs with basePath in Control UI
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #41205 — favicon/apple-touch-icon links in Control UI return 404 when served under a `basePath` (e.g. `/webchat`)
- Adds `injectFaviconBasePath()` helper in `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` that rewrites favicon/apple-touch-icon `href` values at serve time
- Handles both absolute (`/favicon.svg`) and Vite-built relative (`./favicon.svg`) paths
- Passes `basePath` through both `serveResolvedIndexHtml` call sites

## Why only favicons?

Vite's `base: "./"` config rewrites JS/CSS imports to relative paths, which the browser resolves correctly relative to the page URL. The gateway's basePath-stripping logic then serves the correct files from disk. However, hardcoded `<link>` tags in `index.html` for favicons are NOT rewritten by Vite — they remain as `/favicon.svg`, which the browser resolves against the root, skipping the basePath entirely.

## Test plan

- [x] Added 2 unit tests in `gateway-misc.test.ts` (all 23 tests pass)
- [x] Verified `./favicon.svg`, `/favicon.svg`, `./favicon-32.png`, `./apple-touch-icon.png` are all rewritten when basePath is set
- [x] Verified non-favicon hrefs (e.g. `./assets/index.css`) are NOT rewritten
- [x] Verified no-op when basePath is null

### Manual verification

1. Set `basePath` in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`:
   ```json
   {"gateway":{"mode":"local","auth":{"token":"dev-test-token"},"controlUi":{"basePath":"/webchat"}}}
   ```
2. Build and run:
   ```bash
   pnpm build
   OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway
   ```
3. Open `http://localhost:18789/webchat/` in Chrome
4. Verify favicon appears in browser tab
5. Check Network tab: `GET /webchat/favicon.svg` returns 200
6. Check page source: `href="/webchat/favicon.svg"` (not `href="./favicon.svg"`)

### Verification evidence

**curl output confirming rewritten hrefs:**
```
$ curl -s http://localhost:18789/webchat/ | grep favicon
href="/webchat/favicon.svg"
href="/webchat/favicon-32.png"
href="/webchat/apple-touch-icon.png"
```

**Network request: `GET /webchat/favicon.svg` → 200**

### Screenshot

![favicon-fix-verification](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e804ea92-5aa6-441c-a54d-81f8aef3e309)
target.author: zhongnansu

commands.author: bryanpearson

target.title: fix(codex): deliver native spawn_agent completions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-native `spawn_agent` children can finish successfully, but OpenClaw may not mirror their lifecycle, wake the requester session, or deliver the synthesized parent reply visibly back to Discord.
- Why it matters: Mission Control shows stale running task rows and users have to manually poke the parent agent before the completed child result appears.
- What changed: add a Codex-native subagent monitor, a gateway-side transcript/task reconciler, and a completion delivery adapter that turns native child transcript completions into durable parent completion handoffs.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not replace the broader subagent delivery architecture or implement ACP/native `sessions_spawn` ownership policy; it handles Codex harness native `spawn_agent` child threads and their durable transcript completions.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #44925
- Related #52249
- Related #83352
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex-native `spawn_agent` worker completion now wakes the parent Scout session and produces a visible Discord reply without a manual follow-up ping.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw native install on macOS, gateway LaunchAgent, Discord Mission Control channel, Codex harness native `spawn_agent`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Hot-patched the local installed OpenClaw gateway with the same source behavior in this PR.
  2. Restarted the gateway.
  3. From Discord `#mission-control`, asked the parent agent to spawn a worker to check weather using Codex native `spawn_agent`.
  4. Repeated after earlier failed attempts where the worker completed but the parent only replied after a manual poke.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
San Diego: 63°F and cloudy.

Notable forecast: Monday stays mostly cloudy with a high near 70°F, with SW wind gusts up to 20 mph.

Source: NOAA/NWS weather.gov, KSAN observation station.

End-to-end note: this reply is being sent visibly to <#1493670744029401119> from the subagent completion result.

Sent visibly to #mission-control.

Discord receipt: 1505787418349928589
```

Additional live test after packaging: a longer engineer analysis run completed and proactively returned the full worker response in Discord.

- Observed result after fix: the child worker completed, the parent Scout was re-entered without a user poke, and the final synthesized answer was sent visibly to the Discord Mission Control channel.
- What was not tested: Telegram/Slack/ACP paths, Docker install, and upstream CI before PR creation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): repeated pre-fix live runs had completed worker results in the parent context, but the parent did not proactively reply until the user sent another Discord message.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Codex native `spawn_agent` owns the child thread lifecycle inside the Codex harness, so OpenClaw was not reliably converting child transcript terminal events into OpenClaw task completion and parent delivery state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no durable gateway-side reconciler scanned Codex child transcripts for `event_msg.task_complete` / `task_failed` and retried parent completion delivery until the parent produced a visible response.
- Contributing context (if known): the old documented `sessions_spawn`/`sessions_yield` flow is not available in the Codex harness path; native `spawn_agent` produces usable child transcript state, but OpenClaw needs an adapter around it.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-monitor.test.ts`
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-notification.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/codex-native-task-runtime.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: native Codex child notifications and transcript completions create/mirror task rows, finalize terminal rows, mark delivery pending, wake the requester parent, and only mark delivered after a durable direct parent completion response.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the OpenClaw/Codex adapter seams without needing private Discord credentials or a live Codex websocket.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing subagent announce tests covered generic completion delivery but did not cover Codex-native child transcript reconciliation.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-native delegated workers that complete in the background should now proactively deliver their completion back through the parent session, including visible Discord channel replies, instead of waiting for the user to poke the parent agent.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> stale OpenClaw task / no proactive visible reply

After:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> OpenClaw reconciler -> parent completion handoff -> visible Discord reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: native OpenClaw install, gateway LaunchAgent
- Model/provider: Codex harness, OpenAI Codex native `spawn_agent`
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord `#mission-control`
- Relevant config (redacted): Mission Control Discord channel session bound to Scout/parent agent

### Steps

1. From Discord `#mission-control`, ask the parent agent to spawn a worker using native `spawn_agent`.
2. Let the worker complete without sending a follow-up message.
3. Watch for a parent synthesized reply posted visibly in the same Discord channel.
4. Inspect task rows for `codex-thread:<child>` status and delivery status.

### Expected

- Worker completes.
- OpenClaw finalizes the mirrored `codex-native` task row.
- Parent Scout receives a completion handoff.
- Parent reply is sent visibly to Discord.
- Delivery is marked delivered only after the parent response path succeeds.

### Actual

- The live Discord weather test posted visibly to `#mission-control` with Discord receipt `1505787418349928589`.
- A later long engineer analysis run also completed and returned the full response proactively.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Codex-native worker completion posts a visible Discord parent reply after gateway restart; longer engineer analysis run also completed and proactively returned.
- Edge cases checked: stale terminal rows are not blindly replayed; delivery remains pending when the parent handoff does not produce a durable direct completion response; Discord/channel completion uses the message-tool-only path when required.
- What you did **not** verify: ACP runtime, Docker install, non-Discord channels, and full upstream CI.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: duplicate parent replies if a live notification and transcript reconciliation both observe the same child completion.
  - Mitigation: task delivery state and completion dedupe keys prevent delivered rows from being reconciled again; the reconciler ignores already delivered/failed rows and old terminal rows outside the active/pending window.
- Risk: parent transcript completion exists but external channel delivery still fails.
  - Mitigation: Codex-native completions are treated as agent-mediated completion sources and require durable direct parent completion delivery before marking delivered.

target.title: Render Slack progress drafts as plan cards
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve Slack native plan/task-card rendering for progress mode instead of falling back to legacy section/fields blocks.
- Stream native Slack `plan_update` / `task_update` chunks for progress events, including single-tool and concurrent progress updates.
- Use the newest meaningful progress step as the native plan title when no explicit label is configured, while preserving explicit configured labels.
- Finalize in-flight native progress tasks so completed replies do not leave stale `Running` cards.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Slack native progress/task-card rendering remains on the native plan path, source-run streams native Slack progress while work is executing, and finalization clears running task state.
Real environment tested: local source-run OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch, Slack workspace `4ok`, channel `C0ASNARACMT`, infra-engineer-originated message mentioning prod Jules; gateway config used `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` through the source-run Codex auth store.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: restarted the local source-run gateway from `/home/simon/Workspace/openclaw-slack-plan-progress-drafts`, verified `/healthz`, then posted an infra-engineer Slack message with marker `native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928` asking Jules to run exactly `python3 -c "import time; [print('progress-step-%d' % i, flush=True) or time.sleep(3) for i in range(1,6)]; print('FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928')"`; then polled `conversations.replies`, source-run session/trajectory files, source-run gateway log, and `/healthz` after completion.
Evidence after fix: copied live output from the source-run Slack Web API/session-log proof:
```text
parent_ts=1778946569.986069
reply_ts=1778946586.604129
marker=FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928
slack_block_type=plan
task_card={"type":"task_card","task_id":"bash_1","title":"Bash — Bash","status":"complete"}
final_seen=true
plan_message_count=1
stale_running_tasks=[]
false_error_tasks=[]
healthz_before={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
healthz_after={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```
Slack replies for parent ts `1778946569.986069` included one native Slack `plan` block in Jules' reply ts `1778946586.604129`; the plan contained the native task card above; the final reply text contained `FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928`.
Observed result after fix: source-run logs showed Slack inbound for the marker plus `slack-stream` start/appends/stop in channel `C0ASNARACMT` thread `1778946569.986069`; the source-run trajectory ended `status: success` with provider/model `codex/gpt-5.5` / `openai-codex-responses`; no duplicate native progress plan messages were observed, no stale `running`/`in_progress` task remained after completion, and gateway health was `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}` before and after the E2E.
What was not tested: no screenshot/video capture of the transient Slack UI; proof is Slack Web API block state plus source-run gateway logs, session trajectory, and health checks.

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

target.title: feat(cli-output): tool-use injection + rolling-timer indicator for claude-stream-json backends
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** When Claude calls a tool mid-turn on the `claude-cli` backend, the assistant text stream just pauses — often for tens of seconds on `Read` / `Bash` / `Grep` / `WebFetch`. The end user sees a frozen typing indicator and no signal about which tool is running.
- **Why it matters.** Looks like the gateway hung; users abandon turns that are still in flight. Tool calls live in `stream_event` records, never in `assistant_text` deltas, so the existing assistant-stream consumer never surfaces them.
- **What changed.** Two complementary surfaces on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` (gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`):
  1. **In-stream textual marker** via `onAssistantDelta` — `\n\n[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail\n` followed by a rolling `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator that refreshes every 8s, stripped on the next `text_delta` or on turn end.
  2. **Structured `stream: "tool"` agent event** (`{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}`) via a new optional `onToolEvent` callback — consumed by `cli-runner/execute.ts` and forwarded to the agent event bus for downstream tool-tile UIs.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect({backend, providerId})` — no impact on non-Claude CLI backends. Existing `onAssistantDelta` callers are unaffected. `onToolEvent` is opt-in. No tool-output / tool-result event in this PR (the result is reflected in claude's next `text_delta` and the timer-strip handles UX). Tool args sanitized via `sanitizeToolArgs` before either path.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** tool calls during a Claude turn now produce both an in-stream textual marker (with rolling 8s timer indicator) and a structured `stream: "tool"` agent event, when `backend.jsonlDialect === "claude-stream-json"` or `providerId === "claude-cli"`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142, Opus 4.7, live OpenClaw gateway driving the `claude-cli` backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`; live Telegram turn that invokes one or more tools (Read / Bash / Grep / WebFetch); observe the textual marker, the 8s tick refresh, and the marker stripping plus `\n\n` separator when assistant text resumes; verify the `stream: "tool"` agent event in `emitAgentEvent` logs.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Telegram screenshot of a live turn (rolling-timer rendered to the end-user surface): ![telegram rolling-timer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-82285/.proof/telegram-rolling-timer.jpg). Every `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Bash: …` line in the screenshot is one `emitTool` firing in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` against `claude-cli`.
  - Typecheck clean: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`.
  - The markers are an in-flight overlay delivered via `onAssistantDelta`, not persisted to `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl` (which stores claude's structured `result`) or `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` at default verbosity — Telegram is the canonical capture surface.
- **Observed result after fix:** tool calls render as `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail` blocks in the assistant text, with the live timer ticking every 8s. When claude resumes streaming text, the timer is stripped and the text continues from a clean `\n\n` boundary. Agent-event consumers receive a structured `{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}` for each tool call.
- **What was not tested:** end-to-end against a dashboard / external listener for the `stream: "tool"` event surface (event is emitted, visible in `emitAgentEvent` logs, but no downstream UI verified beyond that); Linux/macOS execution (developed on Windows only); bundled MCP tool paths (`server_tool_use` / `mcp_tool_use` content-block types are matched in the tracker but I have not driven a live MCP-tool turn through it); focused unit test for `createClaudeToolUseTracker` is not added yet (see Regression Test Plan).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Adds opt-in `onToolEvent` callback on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` + in-stream textual marker over the existing `onAssistantDelta`.
- Overlap to coordinate: #80046 (CLI tool-event bridge). Maintainers' judgment on whether inline rolling markers ride alongside or after the structured bridge.

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The condition this addresses (no tool-call UX during turns) is a property of the Claude CLI JSONL surface (tool calls live in `stream_event`, never in `assistant_text` deltas), not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` — add a `describe("tool-use tracker", …)` block.
- **Scenario locked in:** simulated `stream_event` sequence — `message_start` → `content_block_start (tool_use, name: "Read")` → 3× `content_block_delta (input_json_delta partial)` → `content_block_stop` — asserts the resulting `ClaudeToolEvent` carries the reassembled `args.file_path`, the right `itemId`, and that the parser's `assistantText` now includes the `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Read: <path>` line.
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode (regression of partial-JSON accumulation, wrong `content_block_stop` dispatch, verbose-gating drift) is fully exercisable by calling the parser's `push()` with a canned JSONL transcript — no live claude needed. Runs in ms.
- **Honest gap:** I have **not** added this test yet. Behaviour is exercised live in my local environment but a frozen-transcript unit test belongs with this PR before flipping out of draft. Flagging as a must-add before un-drafting.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** partial-JSON accumulation in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` could drop or corrupt tool args if `content_block_stop` fires before all `input_json_delta` chunks arrive.
  - **Mitigation:** tracker holds the entry until `content_block_stop`, then parses `entry.input ?? entry.partialJson` via `parseClaudeToolArgs`. The pending unit test (`cli-output.test.ts`) locks in the contract for partial-JSON reassembly.
- **Risk:** rolling-timer `setInterval` leaks if a `content_block_stop` arrives but no subsequent `text_delta` fires (e.g. turn aborts mid-tool).
  - **Mitigation:** `clearToolKeepalive()` is also called on `result`, on `finish()`, and on the next `text_delta`. The parser closure owns the interval handle and explicitly clears it on every terminal path.
- **Risk:** tool args contain sensitive content (file paths, credentials in `WebFetch` URLs, etc.) and surface in both Telegram and agent events.
  - **Mitigation:** `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates and redacts known-sensitive keys before either emission path. Verbose gating (`shouldInjectToolInlineMarkers`) lets sessions opt out of inline markers without losing the structured event.
- **Risk:** the inline marker rides on `onAssistantDelta` — any consumer that strict-parses assistant text as Markdown could choke on the `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator.
  - **Mitigation:** marker uses a deliberately conservative `\n\n[…] 🛠️ …\n` shape that's Markdown-safe (Telegram renders it verbatim). Consumers wanting strict text can ignore by subscribing only to the structured `stream: "tool"` event.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — same allowed-tools set; `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates args before emission.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same tools claude already executes; this PR only renders that they're in flight.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — tracker reads `stream_event` records the parser already processes.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`; non-Claude backends unaffected.
- `onToolEvent` is an additive optional callback — existing parser callers don't need to wire it up.
- No new config, no new env vars. Disable by not subscribing to the new event surface.
